purpose: Production-style start script. Resolves its own directory, checks whether port 3456 (Synapse's SSE server port) already has a listener via `lsof -ti :3456 -sTCP:LISTEN`, refuses to start if Synapse is already running (telling the user to run `./stop.sh` first), and then `cd`s into the script directory and runs `npm run dev`. Functions as a guarded version of launch.sh.
gotchas: Despite being named `start.sh`, it runs `npm run dev` (Vite watch mode), NOT `npm start` (production build). Inconsistent with the `Start Synapse.command` macOS launcher which DOES run `npm start`.
Port-busy check uses port 3456 — this is a Synapse-internal convention. If the SSE server's port is changed in code, this script will silently allow duplicate launches.
Detects only TCP LISTEN state — if the server is in a transient bind state or bound to a different interface, the check can produce false negatives.
Does not source the shell profile — same PATH risk as launch.sh.
Hard-codes port 3456 with no override — there is no SYNAPSE_PORT env var support.
patterns: Port-as-singleton pattern: use a known listening port to detect whether the app is already running, instead of a PID file.
Fail-fast pattern: refuse to start when conflict is detected, with a hint pointing the user at the matching `stop.sh`.
conventions: Captures DIR via `$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)` to get an absolute path safely.
Exit code 1 on conflict, 0 on success.
